text:
INTEGRATED PET/CT EXAMINATION
Whole Body FDG-PET with Diagnostic CT of the Chest, Abdomen, and Pelvis

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Newly diagnosed non-small cell lung carcinoma, adenocarcinoma histology, right upper lobe. EGFR L858R mutation detected. Examination requested for initial staging prior to treatment planning. Patient is a 63-year-old female, former smoker (12 pack-year history, ceased 18 years ago).

TECHNIQUE:
Whole-body PET/CT was performed from the vertex of the skull to the mid-thighs utilizing intravenous F-18 fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG). The patient fasted for a minimum of 6 hours prior to the examination. Serum glucose at time of injection was 94 mg/dL. 10.4 mCi of FDG was administered intravenously. Images were acquired approximately 60 minutes post-injection. CT images were obtained with intravenous iodinated contrast (Omnipaque 350, 100 mL IV, portal venous phase) for the chest, abdomen, and pelvis. Reconstructed slice thickness: 3 mm PET, 2.5 mm CT. Attenuation-corrected and non-attenuation-corrected PET images reviewed in conjunction with fused PET/CT and dedicated CT images in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. CT of the chest acquired at full inspiration.

COMPARISON:
No prior cross-sectional imaging available for comparison.

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:
No pathologically enlarged or hypermetabolic cervical lymph nodes identified. No hypermetabolic head or neck masses. The thyroid gland is homogeneous without focal hypermetabolic nodularity. Parotid and submandibular glands demonstrate symmetric, physiologic FDG uptake. No suspicious osseous lesions in the visualized calvarium or cervical spine.

CHEST:

Primary Tumor:
There is a spiculated, pleural-based mass in the posterior segment of the right upper lobe, measuring 5.8 x 4.2 cm in axial dimensions and approximately 5.1 cm in craniocaudal extent. The mass demonstrates intense and heterogeneous FDG avidity with an SUVmax of 14.3. The internal architecture is heterogeneous, with central areas of slightly lower density that may represent early central necrosis or areas of varying cellular density. The mass demonstrates broad contact with and apparent thickening of the adjacent parietal pleura along the posterior-lateral chest wall, though no definite chest wall invasion or rib destruction is appreciated on the CT component. No associated pleural effusion. The posterior aspect of the mass is in close proximity to the right major fissure. Spiculated margins with associated perilesional inflammatory/desmoplastic change extending into the surrounding lung parenchyma. Air bronchograms are not appreciable within the mass. Satellite nodules are not identified.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The mediastinal contour is normal in configuration. There is no significant mediastinal shift. No pericardial effusion.

Right hilar region: Multiple enlarged lymph nodes at the right hilum are collectively hypermetabolic. The dominant right hilar node measures 1.9 cm in short axis and demonstrates an SUVmax of 7.8. These nodes are conglomerate and partially discrete, with maintained but enlarged fatty hila noted on the left for comparison.

Right paratracheal station (station 4R): There is a cluster of enlarged right paratracheal lymph nodes inferior to the azygos arch, with the dominant node measuring approximately 1.4 cm in short axis and demonstrating an SUVmax of 6.1. These are clearly hypermetabolic above background mediastinal blood pool activity.

Left paratracheal station (station 4L): There is a mildly enlarged and borderline hypermetabolic lymph node at the left paratracheal station measuring approximately 1.1 cm in short axis with an SUVmax of 3.4. This modest activity is above background; while not definitively pathologic by SUV threshold alone, the morphologic enlargement in this clinical context raises concern for contralateral mediastinal nodal involvement and warrants clinical correlation.

Subcarinal station (station 7): No pathologically enlarged or hypermetabolic nodes identified. The subcarinal fat space is preserved.

Aortopulmonary window (station 5/6): No enlarged or hypermetabolic nodes.

Prevascular station (station 3A): Normal-appearing lymph nodes without hypermetabolism.

Posterior mediastinum: No enlarged or hypermetabolic posterior mediastinal nodes. No esophageal abnormality. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without aneurysmal dilation.

Right supraclavicular region: A discrete lymph node measuring approximately 1.3 cm in short axis is identified in the right supraclavicular/low cervical region (station 1R), demonstrating clearly elevated FDG uptake with an SUVmax of 5.2. This is well above mediastinal blood pool and consistent with pathologic nodal involvement. This represents ipsilateral supraclavicular involvement (N3 disease).

Left supraclavicular region: No hypermetabolic or pathologically enlarged nodes.

Lung Parenchyma:
The left lung is clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses in the left lung. No consolidation, ground-glass opacification, or pleural effusion on the left. The right lung parenchyma outside the primary mass demonstrates mild peribronchial thickening in the right lower lobe, likely reactive or related to post-obstructive change given hilar adenopathy. No additional discrete pulmonary nodules or satellite lesions are identified in the right lung beyond the primary mass. No pneumothorax.

Pleural spaces: No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pleural thickening beyond the area of pleural abutment at the primary tumor site.

Airways: The trachea and bilateral main stem bronchi are patent. The right upper lobe bronchus is patent but partially narrowed by extrinsic mass effect and possible endobronchial involvement; formal bronchoscopy not yet performed. No endobronchial lesion identified on CT.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures:
No frank rib destruction or cortical breakthrough to suggest chest wall invasion. No hypermetabolic osseous lesions in the visualized ribs, thoracic vertebrae, sternum, or clavicles. No lytic or blastic lesions. Mild degenerative changes in the thoracic spine without hypermetabolism.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and attenuation. No focal hepatic lesions. No hepatic masses or cystic lesions. No biliary ductal dilation. The liver demonstrates homogeneous background FDG uptake without focal areas of increased activity. No suspicious hepatic lesions.

Spleen: Normal size and morphology. No focal splenic lesions. No hypermetabolic foci.

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic masses or ductal dilation. No hypermetabolic foci.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands are visualized and appear normal. The right adrenal gland has a limb measuring approximately 7 mm and the left adrenal gland is similarly normal in morphology. No adrenal nodules. No focal adrenal FDG uptake above background hepatic activity. No adrenal lesions suspicious for metastatic involvement.

Kidneys: The kidneys are normal in size and contour bilaterally. No hydronephrosis. Bilateral renal cortical cysts are noted (largest on the right, 1.4 cm, simple by CT criteria). FDG is excreted via the urinary tract, with physiologic ureteral and renal pelvis activity noted bilaterally. No renal masses.

Gastrointestinal Tract: Physiologic bowel activity is present throughout the gastrointestinal tract. No bowel wall thickening. No hypermetabolic bowel lesions or masses. Normal appendix. No free intraperitoneal air or fluid.

Abdominal Aorta and Vasculature: The abdominal aorta and major tributaries are patent without aneurysmal change. Mild aortoiliac atherosclerotic calcifications noted, consistent with patient age.

Abdominal and Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No enlarged or hypermetabolic retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic lymph nodes. No para-aortic or aortocaval nodal disease. No iliac nodal enlargement.

Pelvis: The urinary bladder is well-distended with physiologic FDG activity in the urine. The uterus is normal in size without discrete lesions. No pelvic masses. No pelvic free fluid.

OSSEOUS SURVEY (visualized skeleton, vertex to mid-thigh):
No hypermetabolic osseous foci suspicious for skeletal metastases. No lytic or blastic lesions identified in the visualized axial or appendicular skeleton. Mild multilevel degenerative changes in the lumbar spine, including endplate osteophytosis and mild loss of disc height at L3-L4 and L4-L5, without associated hypermetabolism. No pathological fractures.

SOFT TISSUES:
No abnormal soft tissue masses or hypermetabolic foci in the chest wall or peripheral soft tissues. No subcutaneous nodularity.

IMPRESSION:

1. HYPERMETABOLIC RIGHT UPPER LOBE MASS (5.8 x 4.2 cm, SUVmax 14.3): Large spiculated, pleural-based mass in the posterior segment of the right upper lobe demonstrating intense FDG avidity, consistent with known primary lung adenocarcinoma. The mass abuts and thickens the adjacent parietal pleura; frank chest wall invasion is not definitively demonstrated.

2. IPSILATERAL HILAR ADENOPATHY (N1): Multiple hypermetabolic right hilar lymph nodes, dominant node 1.9 cm, SUVmax 7.8, consistent with nodal metastatic involvement.

3. IPSILATERAL MEDIASTINAL ADENOPATHY (N2): Hypermetabolic right paratracheal (4R) lymph nodes measuring up to 1.4 cm, SUVmax 6.1, consistent with ipsilateral mediastinal nodal involvement (N2 disease).

4. CONTRALATERAL MEDIASTINAL INVOLVEMENT (N3): Borderline hypermetabolic and enlarged left paratracheal (4L) lymph node, SUVmax 3.4. In the context of the current primary tumor and ipsilateral nodal disease, this finding is highly suspicious for contralateral mediastinal involvement, upgrading nodal status to N3. Pathologic confirmation may be considered.

5. IPSILATERAL SUPRACLAVICULAR ADENOPATHY (N3): Clearly hypermetabolic right supraclavicular lymph node, 1.3 cm, SUVmax 5.2, consistent with supraclavicular nodal involvement (N3 disease by AJCC 8th edition, ipsilateral).

6. NO DISTANT METASTATIC DISEASE (M0): No hepatic, adrenal, osseous, or distant soft tissue lesions to suggest systemic hematogenous spread. The brain was not imaged on this examination; dedicated brain MRI with gadolinium is recommended to complete the staging evaluation given the high nodal burden and known adenocarcinoma histology with EGFR mutation.

OVERALL STAGING IMPRESSION: Findings are consistent with at minimum Stage IIIB non-small cell lung carcinoma (T3 N3 M0) based on the combination of primary tumor size with pleural abutment, ipsilateral supraclavicular nodal involvement, and contralateral mediastinal borderline nodal involvement. Brain MRI recommended to exclude intracranial metastases prior to definitive treatment planning.

Report reviewed and electronically signed.
Attending Radiologist: [Name], MD
Division of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging / Thoracic Radiology
